faith.summary: Plibersek has said she was raised Catholic. In her 2022 Archbishop Daniel Mannix Lecture she said 'I was raised firmly within the arms of the Catholic Church,' describing her first communion, confirmation, Sunday mass and weekly confession, and adding that 'growing up Catholic has influenced my politics.' She does not state whether she currently practises.

sources.title: Speech – Tanya Plibersek – The 38th Archbishop Daniel Mannix Lecture – Melbourne – Wednesday, 23 March 2022

immigration.summary: In a 2003 workshop talk on multiculturalism, Plibersek argued that "the first and biggest lie is the one that suggests asylum seekers are a threat to this nation," noting Australia accepts a small number of refugees by world standards. She criticised Australia for choosing "the most expensive system for dealing with refugees - locking them up - or even more expensive, putting them on Nauru or Manus Island," and is a vocal advocate for multiculturalism.

sources.title: Tanya Plibersek, ALP Member for Sydney: About Multiculturalism in Australia

immigration.summary: In her 2025 CPAC speech, McKenzie said she is "pro-migrant and against aggressive immigration," warning against an immigration program that strains housing, infrastructure, services and social cohesion. She said Australia should welcome migrants who share its values and respect the rule of law, stated "We do not need Islamists in this country," and noted Australia has avoided the illegal-immigration problems seen elsewhere partly due to tough decisions by previous Coalition governments.
